verses.kjv: THESE be the words which Moses spake unto all Israel on this side Jordan in the wilderness, in the plain over against the Red sea, between Paran, and Tophel, and Laban, and Hazeroth, and Dizahab.
verses.bsb: These are the words that Moses spoke to all Israel in the wilderness east of the Jordan—in the Arabah opposite Suph—between Paran and Tophel, Laban, Hazeroth, and Dizahab.

verses.kjv: (There are eleven days’ journey from Horeb by the way of mount Seir unto Kadesh-barnea.)
verses.bsb: It is an eleven-day journey from Horeb to Kadesh-barnea by way of Mount Seir.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass in the fortieth year, in the eleventh month, on the first day of the month, that Moses spake unto the children of Israel, according unto all that the Lord had given him in commandment unto them;
verses.bsb: In the fortieth year, on the first day of the eleventh month, Moses proclaimed to the Israelites all that the LORD had commanded him concerning them.

verses.kjv: After he had slain Sihon the king of the Amorites, which dwelt in Heshbon, and Og the king of Bashan, which dwelt at Astaroth in Edrei:
verses.bsb: This was after he had defeated Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon, and then at Edrei had defeated Og king of Bashan, who lived in Ashtaroth.

verses.kjv: On this side Jordan, in the land of Moab, began Moses to declare this law, saying,
verses.bsb: On the east side of the Jordan in the land of Moab, Moses began to explain this law, saying:

verses.kjv: The Lord our God spake unto us in Horeb, saying, Ye have dwelt long enough in this mount:
verses.bsb: The LORD our God said to us at Horeb: “You have stayed at this mountain long enough.

verses.kjv: Turn you, and take your journey, and go to the mount of the Amorites, and unto all the places nigh thereunto, in the plain, in the hills, and in the vale, and in the south, and by the sea side, to the land of the Canaanites, and unto Lebanon, unto the great river, the river Euphrates.
verses.bsb: Resume your journey and go to the hill country of the Amorites; go to all the neighboring peoples in the Arabah, in the hill country, in the foothills, in the Negev, and along the seacoast to the land of the Canaanites and to Lebanon, as far as the great River Euphrates.

verses.kjv: Behold, I have set the land before you: go in and possess the land which the Lord sware unto your fathers, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, to give unto them and to their seed after them.
verses.bsb: See, I have placed the land before you. Enter and possess the land that the LORD swore He would give to your fathers Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and to their descendants after them.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And I spake unto you at that time, saying, I am not able to bear you myself alone:
verses.bsb: At that time I said to you, “I cannot carry the burden for you alone.

verses.kjv: The Lord your God hath multiplied you, and, behold, ye are this day as the stars of heaven for multitude.
verses.bsb: The LORD your God has multiplied you, so that today you are as numerous as the stars in the sky.

verses.kjv: (The Lord God of your fathers make you a thousand times so many more as ye are, and bless you, as he hath promised you!)
verses.bsb: May the LORD, the God of your fathers, increase you a thousand times over and bless you as He has promised.

verses.kjv: How can I myself alone bear your cumbrance, and your burden, and your strife?
verses.bsb: But how can I bear your troubles, burdens, and disputes all by myself?

verses.kjv: Take you wise men, and understanding, and known among your tribes, and I will make them rulers over you.
verses.bsb: Choose for yourselves wise, understanding, and respected men from each of your tribes, and I will appoint them as your leaders.”

verses.kjv: And ye answered me, and said, The thing which thou hast spoken is good for us to do.
verses.bsb: And you answered me and said, “What you propose to do is good.”

verses.kjv: So I took the chief of your tribes, wise men, and known, and made them heads over you, captains over thousands, and captains over hundreds, and captains over fifties, and captains over tens, and officers among your tribes.
verses.bsb: So I took the leaders of your tribes, wise and respected men, and appointed them as leaders over you—as commanders of thousands, of hundreds, of fifties, and of tens, and as officers for your tribes.

verses.kjv: And I charged your judges at that time, saying, Hear the causes between your brethren, and judge righteously between every man and his brother, and the stranger that is with him.
verses.bsb: At that time I charged your judges: “Hear the disputes between your brothers, and judge fairly between a man and his brother or a foreign resident.

verses.kjv: Ye shall not respect persons in judgment; but ye shall hear the small as well as the great; ye shall not be afraid of the face of man; for the judgment is God’s: and the cause that is too hard for you, bring it unto me, and I will hear it.
verses.bsb: Show no partiality in judging; hear both small and great alike. Do not be intimidated by anyone, for judgment belongs to God. And bring to me any case too difficult for you, and I will hear it.”

verses.kjv: And I commanded you at that time all the things which ye should do.
verses.bsb: And at that time I commanded you all the things you were to do.

verses.kjv: ¶ And when we departed from Horeb, we went through all that great and terrible wilderness, which ye saw by the way of the mountain of the Amorites, as the Lord our God commanded us; and we came to Kadesh-barnea.
verses.bsb: And just as the LORD our God had commanded us, we set out from Horeb and went toward the hill country of the Amorites, through all the vast and terrifying wilderness you have seen. When we reached Kadesh-barnea,

verses.kjv: And I said unto you, Ye are come unto the mountain of the Amorites, which the Lord our God doth give unto us.
verses.bsb: I said: “You have reached the hill country of the Amorites, which the LORD our God is giving us.

verses.kjv: Behold, the Lord thy God hath set the land before thee: go up and possess it, as the Lord God of thy fathers hath said unto thee; fear not, neither be discouraged.
verses.bsb: See, the LORD your God has placed the land before you. Go up and take possession of it as the LORD, the God of your fathers, has told you. Do not be afraid or discouraged.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And ye came near unto me every one of you, and said, We will send men before us, and they shall search us out the land, and bring us word again by what way we must go up, and into what cities we shall come.
verses.bsb: Then all of you approached me and said, “Let us send men ahead of us to search out the land and bring us word of what route to follow and which cities to enter.”

verses.kjv: And the saying pleased me well: and I took twelve men of you, one of a tribe:
verses.bsb: The plan seemed good to me, so I selected twelve men from among you, one from each tribe.

verses.kjv: And they turned and went up into the mountain, and came unto the valley of Eshcol, and searched it out.
verses.bsb: They left and went up into the hill country, and came to the Valley of Eshcol and spied out the land.

verses.kjv: And they took of the fruit of the land in their hands, and brought it down unto us, and brought us word again, and said, It is a good land which the Lord our God doth give us.
verses.bsb: They took some of the fruit of the land in their hands, carried it down to us, and brought us word: “It is a good land that the LORD our God is giving us.”

verses.kjv: Notwithstanding ye would not go up, but rebelled against the commandment of the Lord your God:
verses.bsb: But you were unwilling to go up; you rebelled against the command of the LORD your God.

verses.kjv: And ye murmured in your tents, and said, Because the Lord hated us, he hath brought us forth out of the land of Egypt, to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites, to destroy us.
verses.bsb: You grumbled in your tents and said, “Because the LORD hates us, He has brought us out of the land of Egypt to deliver us into the hand of the Amorites to be annihilated.

verses.kjv: Whither shall we go up? our brethren have discouraged our heart, saying, The people is greater and taller than we; the cities are great and walled up to heaven; and moreover we have seen the sons of the Anakims there.
verses.bsb: Where can we go? Our brothers have made our hearts melt, saying: ‘The people are larger and taller than we are; the cities are large, with walls up to the heavens. We even saw the descendants of the Anakim there.’”

verses.kjv: Then I said unto you, Dread not, neither be afraid of them.
verses.bsb: So I said to you: “Do not be terrified or afraid of them!

verses.kjv: The Lord your God which goeth before you, he shall fight for you, according to all that he did for you in Egypt before your eyes;
verses.bsb: The LORD your God, who goes before you, will fight for you, just as you saw Him do for you in Egypt

verses.kjv: And in the wilderness, where thou hast seen how that the Lord thy God bare thee, as a man doth bear his son, in all the way that ye went, until ye came into this place.
verses.bsb: and in the wilderness, where the LORD your God carried you, as a man carries his son, all the way by which you traveled until you reached this place.”

verses.kjv: Yet in this thing ye did not believe the Lord your God,
verses.bsb: But in spite of all this, you did not trust the LORD your God,

verses.kjv: Who went in the way before you, to search you out a place to pitch your tents in, in fire by night, to shew you by what way ye should go, and in a cloud by day.
verses.bsb: who went before you on the journey, in the fire by night and in the cloud by day, to seek out a place for you to camp and to show you the road to travel.

verses.kjv: And the Lord heard the voice of your words, and was wroth, and sware, saying,
verses.bsb: When the LORD heard your words, He grew angry and swore an oath, saying,

verses.kjv: Surely there shall not one of these men of this evil generation see that good land, which I sware to give unto your fathers,
verses.bsb: “Not one of the men of this evil generation shall see the good land I swore to give your fathers,

verses.kjv: Save Caleb the son of Jephunneh; he shall see it, and to him will I give the land that he hath trodden upon, and to his children, because he hath wholly followed the Lord.
verses.bsb: except Caleb son of Jephunneh. He will see it, and I will give him and his descendants the land on which he has set foot, because he followed the LORD wholeheartedly.”

verses.kjv: Also the Lord was angry with me for your sakes, saying, Thou also shalt not go in thither.
verses.bsb: The LORD was also angry with me on your account, and He said, “Not even you shall enter the land.

verses.kjv: But Joshua the son of Nun, which standeth before thee, he shall go in thither: encourage him: for he shall cause Israel to inherit it.
verses.bsb: Joshua son of Nun, who stands before you, will enter it. Encourage him, for he will enable Israel to inherit the land.

verses.kjv: Moreover your little ones, which ye said should be a prey, and your children, which in that day had no knowledge between good and evil, they shall go in thither, and unto them will I give it, and they shall possess it.
verses.bsb: And the little ones you said would become captives—your children who on that day did not know good from evil—will enter the land that I will give them, and they will possess it.

verses.kjv: But as for you, turn you, and take your journey into the wilderness by the way of the Red sea.
verses.bsb: But you are to turn back and head for the wilderness along the route to the Red Sea.”

verses.kjv: Then ye answered and said unto me, We have sinned against the Lord, we will go up and fight, according to all that the Lord our God commanded us. And when ye had girded on every man his weapons of war, ye were ready to go up into the hill.
verses.bsb: “We have sinned against the LORD,” you replied. “We will go up and fight, as the LORD our God has commanded us.” Then each of you put on his weapons of war, thinking it easy to go up into the hill country.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto me, Say unto them, Go not up, neither fight; for I am not among you; lest ye be smitten before your enemies.
verses.bsb: But the LORD said to me, “Tell them not to go up and fight, for I am not with you to keep you from defeat by your enemies.”

verses.kjv: So I spake unto you; and ye would not hear, but rebelled against the commandment of the Lord, and went presumptuously up into the hill.
verses.bsb: So I spoke to you, but you would not listen. You rebelled against the command of the LORD and presumptuously went up into the hill country.

verses.kjv: And the Amorites, which dwelt in that mountain, came out against you, and chased you, as bees do, and destroyed you in Seir, even unto Hormah.
verses.bsb: Then the Amorites who lived in the hills came out against you and chased you like a swarm of bees. They routed you from Seir all the way to Hormah.

verses.kjv: And ye returned and wept before the Lord; but the Lord would not hearken to your voice, nor give ear unto you.
verses.bsb: And you returned and wept before the LORD, but He would not listen to your voice or give ear to you.

verses.kjv: So ye abode in Kadesh many days, according unto the days that ye abode there.
verses.bsb: For this reason you stayed in Kadesh for a long time—a very long time.

verses.kjv: THEN we turned, and took our journey into the wilderness by the way of the Red sea, as the Lord spake unto me: and we compassed mount Seir many days.
verses.bsb: Then we turned back and headed for the wilderness by way of the Red Sea, as the LORD had instructed me, and for many days we wandered around Mount Seir.

verses.kjv: And the Lord spake unto me, saying,
verses.bsb: At this time the LORD said to me,

verses.kjv: Ye have compassed this mountain long enough: turn you northward.
verses.bsb: “You have been wandering around this hill country long enough; turn to the north

verses.kjv: And command thou the people, saying, Ye are to pass through the coast of your brethren the children of Esau, which dwell in Seir; and they shall be afraid of you: take ye good heed unto yourselves therefore:
verses.bsb: and command the people: ‘You will pass through the territory of your brothers, the descendants of Esau, who live in Seir. They will be afraid of you, so you must be very careful.

verses.kjv: Meddle not with them; for I will not give you of their land, no, not so much as a foot breadth; because I have given mount Seir unto Esau for a possession.
verses.bsb: Do not provoke them, for I will not give you any of their land, not even a footprint, because I have given Mount Seir to Esau as his possession.

verses.kjv: Ye shall buy meat of them for money, that ye may eat; and ye shall also buy water of them for money, that ye may drink.
verses.bsb: You are to pay them in silver for the food you eat and the water you drink.’”

verses.kjv: For the Lord thy God hath blessed thee in all the works of thy hand: he knoweth thy walking through this great wilderness: these forty years the Lord thy God hath been with thee; thou hast lacked nothing.
verses.bsb: Indeed, the LORD your God has blessed you in all the work of your hands. He has watched over your journey through this vast wilderness. The LORD your God has been with you these forty years, and you have lacked nothing.

verses.kjv: And when we passed by from our brethren the children of Esau, which dwelt in Seir, through the way of the plain from Elath, and from Ezion-gaber, we turned and passed by the way of the wilderness of Moab.
verses.bsb: So we passed by our brothers, the descendants of Esau, who live in Seir. We turned away from the Arabah road, which comes up from Elath and Ezion-geber, and traveled along the road of the Wilderness of Moab.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto me, Distress not the Moabites, neither contend with them in battle: for I will not give thee of their land for a possession; because I have given Ar unto the children of Lot for a possession.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to me, “Do not harass the Moabites or provoke them to war, for I will not give you any of their land, because I have given Ar to the descendants of Lot as their possession.”

verses.kjv: The Emims dwelt therein in times past, a people great, and many, and tall, as the Anakims;
verses.bsb: (The Emites used to live there, a people great and many, as tall as the Anakites.

verses.kjv: Which also were accounted giants, as the Anakims; but the Moabites call them Emims.
verses.bsb: Like the Anakites, they were also regarded as Rephaim, though the Moabites called them Emites.

verses.kjv: The Horims also dwelt in Seir beforetime; but the children of Esau succeeded them, when they had destroyed them from before them, and dwelt in their stead; as Israel did unto the land of his possession, which the Lord gave unto them.
verses.bsb: The Horites used to live in Seir, but the descendants of Esau drove them out. They destroyed the Horites from before them and settled in their place, just as Israel did in the land that the LORD gave them as their possession.)

verses.kjv: Now rise up, said I, and get you over the brook Zered. And we went over the brook Zered.
verses.bsb: “Now arise and cross over the Brook of Zered.”  So we crossed over the Brook of Zered.

verses.kjv: And the space in which we came from Kadesh-barnea, until we were come over the brook Zered, was thirty and eight years; until all the generation of the men of war were wasted out from among the host, as the Lord sware unto them.
verses.bsb: The time we spent traveling from Kadesh-barnea until we crossed over the Brook of Zered was thirty-eight years, until that entire generation of fighting men had perished from the camp, as the LORD had sworn to them.

verses.kjv: For indeed the hand of the Lord was against them, to destroy them from among the host, until they were consumed.
verses.bsb: Indeed, the LORD’s hand was against them, to eliminate them from the camp, until they had all perished.

verses.kjv: ¶ So it came to pass, when all the men of war were consumed and dead from among the people,
verses.bsb: Now when all the fighting men among the people had died,

verses.kjv: That the Lord spake unto me, saying,
verses.bsb: the LORD said to me,

verses.kjv: Thou art to pass over through Ar, the coast of Moab, this day:
verses.bsb: “Today you are going to cross the border of Moab at Ar.

verses.kjv: And when thou comest nigh over against the children of Ammon, distress them not, nor meddle with them: for I will not give thee of the land of the children of Ammon any possession; because I have given it unto the children of Lot for a possession.
verses.bsb: But when you get close to the Ammonites, do not harass them or provoke them, for I will not give you any of the land of the Ammonites. I have given it to the descendants of Lot as their possession.”

verses.kjv: (That also was accounted a land of giants: giants dwelt therein in old time; and the Ammonites call them Zamzummims;
verses.bsb: (That too was regarded as the land of the Rephaim, who used to live there, though the Ammonites called them Zamzummites.

verses.kjv: A people great, and many, and tall, as the Anakims; but the Lord destroyed them before them; and they succeeded them, and dwelt in their stead:
verses.bsb: They were a people great and many, as tall as the Anakites. But the LORD destroyed them from before the Ammonites, who drove them out and settled in their place,

verses.kjv: As he did to the children of Esau, which dwelt in Seir, when he destroyed the Horims from before them; and they succeeded them, and dwelt in their stead even unto this day:
verses.bsb: just as He had done for the descendants of Esau who lived in Seir, when He destroyed the Horites from before them. They drove them out and have lived in their place to this day.

verses.kjv: And the Avims which dwelt in Hazerim, even unto Azzah, the Caphtorims, which came forth out of Caphtor, destroyed them, and dwelt in their stead.)
verses.bsb: And the Avvim, who lived in villages as far as Gaza, were destroyed by the Caphtorites, who came out of Caphtor and settled in their place.)

verses.kjv: ¶ Rise ye up, take your journey, and pass over the river Arnon: behold, I have given into thine hand Sihon the Amorite, king of Heshbon, and his land: begin to possess it, and contend with him in battle.
verses.bsb: “Arise, set out, and cross the Arnon Valley. See, I have delivered into your hand Sihon the Amorite, king of Heshbon, and his land. Begin to take possession of it and engage him in battle.

verses.kjv: This day will I begin to put the dread of thee and the fear of thee upon the nations that are under the whole heaven, who shall hear report of thee, and shall tremble, and be in anguish because of thee.
verses.bsb: This very day I will begin to put the dread and fear of you upon all the nations under heaven. They will hear the reports of you and tremble in anguish because of you.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And I sent messengers out of the wilderness of Kedemoth unto Sihon king of Heshbon with words of peace, saying,
verses.bsb: So from the Wilderness of Kedemoth I sent messengers with an offer of peace to Sihon king of Heshbon, saying,

verses.kjv: Let me pass through thy land: I will go along by the high way, I will neither turn unto the right hand nor to the left.
verses.bsb: “Let us pass through your land; we will stay on the main road. We will not turn to the right or to the left.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt sell me meat for money, that I may eat; and give me water for money, that I may drink: only I will pass through on my feet;
verses.bsb: You can sell us food to eat and water to drink in exchange for silver. Only let us pass through on foot,

verses.kjv: (As the children of Esau which dwell in Seir, and the Moabites which dwell in Ar, did unto me;) until I shall pass over Jordan into the land which the Lord our God giveth us.
verses.bsb: just as the descendants of Esau who live in Seir and the Moabites who live in Ar did for us, until we cross the Jordan into the land that the LORD our God is giving us.”

verses.kjv: But Sihon king of Heshbon would not let us pass by him: for the Lord thy God hardened his spirit, and made his heart obstinate, that he might deliver him into thy hand, as appeareth this day.
verses.bsb: But Sihon king of Heshbon would not let us pass through, for the LORD your God had made his spirit stubborn and his heart obstinate, that He might deliver him into your hand, as is the case this day.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto me, Behold, I have begun to give Sihon and his land before thee: begin to possess, that thou mayest inherit his land.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to me, “See, I have begun to deliver Sihon and his land over to you. Now begin to conquer and possess his land.”

verses.kjv: Then Sihon came out against us, he and all his people, to fight at Jahaz.
verses.bsb: So Sihon and his whole army came out for battle against us at Jahaz.

verses.kjv: And the Lord our God delivered him before us; and we smote him, and his sons, and all his people.
verses.bsb: And the LORD our God delivered him over to us, and we defeated him and his sons and his whole army.

verses.kjv: And we took all his cities at that time, and utterly destroyed the men, and the women, and the little ones, of every city, we left none to remain:
verses.bsb: At that time we captured all his cities and devoted to destruction the people of every city, including women and children. We left no survivors.

verses.kjv: Only the cattle we took for a prey unto ourselves, and the spoil of the cities which we took.
verses.bsb: We carried off for ourselves only the livestock and the plunder from the cities we captured.

verses.kjv: From Aroer, which is by the brink of the river of Arnon, and from the city that is by the river, even unto Gilead, there was not one city too strong for us: the Lord our God delivered all unto us:
verses.bsb: From Aroer on the rim of the Arnon Valley, along with the city in the valley, even as far as Gilead, not one city had walls too high for us. The LORD our God gave us all of them.

verses.kjv: Only unto the land of the children of Ammon thou camest not, nor unto any place of the river Jabbok, nor unto the cities in the mountains, nor unto whatsoever the Lord our God forbad us.
verses.bsb: But you did not go near the land of the Ammonites, or the land along the banks of the Jabbok River, or the cities of the hill country, or any place that the LORD our God had forbidden.

verses.kjv: THEN we turned, and went up the way to Bashan: and Og the king of Bashan came out against us, he and all his people, to battle at Edrei.
verses.bsb: Then we turned and went up the road to Bashan, and Og king of Bashan and his whole army came out to meet us in battle at Edrei.

verses.kjv: And the Lord said unto me, Fear him not: for I will deliver him, and all his people, and his land, into thy hand; and thou shalt do unto him as thou didst unto Sihon king of the Amorites, which dwelt at Heshbon.
verses.bsb: But the LORD said to me, “Do not fear him, for I have delivered him into your hand, along with all his people and his land. Do to him as you did to Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon.”

verses.kjv: So the Lord our God delivered into our hands Og also, the king of Bashan, and all his people: and we smote him until none was left to him remaining.
verses.bsb: So the LORD our God also delivered Og king of Bashan and his whole army into our hands. We struck them down until no survivor was left.

verses.kjv: And we took all his cities at that time, there was not a city which we took not from them, threescore cities, all the region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan.
verses.bsb: At that time we captured all sixty of his cities. There was not a single city we failed to take—the entire region of Argob, the kingdom of Og in Bashan.

verses.kjv: All these cities were fenced with high walls, gates, and bars; beside unwalled towns a great many.
verses.bsb: All these cities were fortified with high walls and gates and bars, and there were many more unwalled villages.

verses.kjv: And we utterly destroyed them, as we did unto Sihon king of Heshbon, utterly destroying the men, women, and children, of every city.
verses.bsb: We devoted them to destruction, as we had done to Sihon king of Heshbon, utterly destroying the men, women, and children of every city.

verses.kjv: But all the cattle, and the spoil of the cities, we took for a prey to ourselves.
verses.bsb: But all the livestock and plunder of the cities we carried off for ourselves.

verses.kjv: And we took at that time out of the hand of the two kings of the Amorites the land that was on this side Jordan, from the river of Arnon unto mount Hermon;
verses.bsb: At that time we took from the two kings of the Amorites the land across the Jordan, from the Arnon Valley as far as Mount Hermon—

verses.kjv: (Which Hermon the Sidonians call Sirion; and the Amorites call it Shenir;)
verses.bsb: which the Sidonians call Sirion but the Amorites call Senir—

verses.kjv: All the cities of the plain, and all Gilead, and all Bashan, unto Salchah and Edrei, cities of the kingdom of Og in Bashan.
verses.bsb: all the cities of the plateau, all of Gilead, and all of Bashan as far as the cities of Salecah and Edrei in the kingdom of Og.

verses.kjv: For only Og king of Bashan remained of the remnant of giants; behold, his bedstead was a bedstead of iron; is it not in Rabbath of the children of Ammon? nine cubits was the length thereof, and four cubits the breadth of it, after the cubit of a man.
verses.bsb: (For only Og king of Bashan had remained of the remnant of the Rephaim. His bed of iron, nine cubits long and four cubits wide, is still in Rabbah of the Ammonites.)

verses.kjv: And this land, which we possessed at that time, from Aroer, which is by the river Arnon, and half mount Gilead, and the cities thereof, gave I unto the Reubenites and to the Gadites.
verses.bsb: So at that time we took possession of this land. To the Reubenites and Gadites I gave the land beyond Aroer along the Arnon Valley, and half the hill country of Gilead, along with its cities.

verses.kjv: And the rest of Gilead, and all Bashan, being the kingdom of Og, gave I unto the half tribe of Manasseh; all the region of Argob, with all Bashan, which was called the land of giants.
verses.bsb: To the half-tribe of Manasseh I gave the rest of Gilead and all of Bashan, the kingdom of Og. (The entire region of Argob, the whole territory of Bashan, used to be called the land of the Rephaim.)

verses.kjv: Jair the son of Manasseh took all the country of Argob unto the coasts of Geshuri and Maachathi; and called them after his own name, Bashan-havoth-jair, unto this day.
verses.bsb: Jair, a descendant of Manasseh, took the whole region of Argob as far as the border of the Geshurites and Maacathites. He renamed Bashan after himself, Havvoth-jair, by which it is called to this day.

verses.kjv: And I gave Gilead unto Machir.
verses.bsb: To Machir I gave Gilead,

verses.kjv: And unto the Reubenites and unto the Gadites I gave from Gilead even unto the river Arnon half the valley, and the border even unto the river Jabbok, which is the border of the children of Ammon;
verses.bsb: and to the Reubenites and Gadites I gave the territory from Gilead to the Arnon Valley (the middle of the valley was the border) and up to the Jabbok River, the border of the Ammonites.

verses.kjv: The plain also, and Jordan, and the coast thereof, from Chinnereth even unto the sea of the plain, even the salt sea, under Ashdoth-pisgah eastward.
verses.bsb: The Jordan River in the Arabah bordered it from Chinnereth to the Sea of the Arabah (the Salt Sea) with the slopes of Pisgah to the east.

verses.kjv: ¶ And I commanded you at that time, saying, The Lord your God hath given you this land to possess it: ye shall pass over armed before your brethren the children of Israel, all that are meet for the war.
verses.bsb: At that time I commanded you: “The LORD your God has given you this land to possess. All your men of valor are to cross over, armed for battle, ahead of your brothers, the Israelites.

verses.kjv: But your wives, and your little ones, and your cattle, (for I know that ye have much cattle,) shall abide in your cities which I have given you;
verses.bsb: But your wives, your children, and your livestock—I know that you have much livestock—may remain in the cities I have given you,

verses.kjv: Until the Lord have given rest unto your brethren, as well as unto you, and until they also possess the land which the Lord your God hath given them beyond Jordan: and then shall ye return every man unto his possession, which I have given you.
verses.bsb: until the LORD gives rest to your brothers as He has to you, and they too have taken possession of the land that the LORD your God is giving them across the Jordan. Then each of you may return to the possession I have given you.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And I commanded Joshua at that time, saying, Thine eyes have seen all that the Lord your God hath done unto these two kings: so shall the Lord do unto all the kingdoms whither thou passest.
verses.bsb: And at that time I commanded Joshua: “Your own eyes have seen all that the LORD your God has done to these two kings. The LORD will do the same to all the kingdoms you are about to enter.

verses.kjv: Ye shall not fear them: for the Lord your God he shall fight for you.
verses.bsb: Do not be afraid of them, for the LORD your God Himself will fight for you.”

verses.kjv: And I besought the Lord at that time, saying,
verses.bsb: At that time I also pleaded with the LORD:

verses.kjv: O Lord God, thou hast begun to shew thy servant thy greatness, and thy mighty hand: for what God is there in heaven or in earth, that can do according to thy works, and according to thy might?
verses.bsb: “O Lord GOD, You have begun to show Your greatness and power to Your servant. For what god in heaven or on earth can perform such works and mighty acts as Yours?

verses.kjv: I pray thee, let me go over, and see the good land that is beyond Jordan, that goodly mountain, and Lebanon.
verses.bsb: Please let me cross over and see the good land beyond the Jordan—that pleasant hill country as well as Lebanon!”

verses.kjv: But the Lord was wroth with me for your sakes, and would not hear me: and the Lord said unto me, Let it suffice thee; speak no more unto me of this matter.
verses.bsb: But the LORD was angry with me on account of you, and He would not listen to me. “That is enough,” the LORD said to me. “Do not speak to Me again about this matter.

verses.kjv: Get thee up into the top of Pisgah, and lift up thine eyes westward, and northward, and southward, and eastward, and behold it with thine eyes: for thou shalt not go over this Jordan.
verses.bsb: Go to the top of Pisgah and look to the west and north and south and east. See the land with your own eyes, for you will not cross this Jordan.

verses.kjv: But charge Joshua, and encourage him, and strengthen him: for he shall go over before this people, and he shall cause them to inherit the land which thou shalt see.
verses.bsb: But commission Joshua, encourage him, and strengthen him, for he will cross over ahead of the people and enable them to inherit the land that you will see.”

verses.kjv: So we abode in the valley over against Beth-peor.
verses.bsb: So we stayed in the valley opposite Beth-peor.

verses.kjv: NOW therefore hearken, O Israel, unto the statutes and unto the judgments, which I teach you, for to do them, that ye may live, and go in and possess the land which the Lord God of your fathers giveth you.
verses.bsb: Hear now, O Israel, the statutes and ordinances I am teaching you to follow, so that you may live and may enter and take possession of the land that the LORD, the God of your fathers, is giving you.

verses.kjv: Ye shall not add unto the word which I command you, neither shall ye diminish ought from it, that ye may keep the commandments of the Lord your God which I command you.
verses.bsb: You must not add to or subtract from what I command you, so that you may keep the commandments of the LORD your God that I am giving you.

verses.kjv: Your eyes have seen what the Lord did because of Baal-peor: for all the men that followed Baal-peor, the Lord thy God hath destroyed them from among you.
verses.bsb: Your eyes have seen what the LORD did at Baal-peor, for the LORD your God destroyed from among you all who followed Baal of Peor.

verses.kjv: But ye that did cleave unto the Lord your God are alive every one of you this day.
verses.bsb: But you who held fast to the LORD your God are alive to this day, every one of you.

verses.kjv: Behold, I have taught you statutes and judgments, even as the Lord my God commanded me, that ye should do so in the land whither ye go to possess it.
verses.bsb: See, I have taught you statutes and ordinances just as the LORD my God has commanded me, so that you may follow them in the land that you are about to enter and possess.

verses.kjv: Keep therefore and do them; for this is your wisdom and your understanding in the sight of the nations, which shall hear all these statutes, and say, Surely this great nation is a wise and understanding people.
verses.bsb: Observe them carefully, for this will show your wisdom and understanding in the sight of the peoples, who will hear of all these statutes and say, “Surely this great nation is a wise and understanding people.”

verses.kjv: For what nation is there so great, who hath God so nigh unto them, as the Lord our God is in all things that we call upon him for?
verses.bsb: For what nation is great enough to have a god as near to them as the LORD our God is to us whenever we call on Him?

verses.kjv: And what nation is there so great, that hath statutes and judgments so righteous as all this law, which I set before you this day?
verses.bsb: And what nation is great enough to have righteous statutes and ordinances like this entire law I set before you today?

verses.kjv: Only take heed to thyself, and keep thy soul diligently, lest thou forget the things which thine eyes have seen, and lest they depart from thy heart all the days of thy life: but teach them thy sons, and thy sons’ sons;
verses.bsb: Only be on your guard and diligently watch yourselves, so that you do not forget the things your eyes have seen, and so that they do not slip from your heart as long as you live. Teach them to your children and grandchildren.

verses.kjv: Specially the day that thou stoodest before the Lord thy God in Horeb, when the Lord said unto me, Gather me the people together, and I will make them hear my words, that they may learn to fear me all the days that they shall live upon the earth, and that they may teach their children.
verses.bsb: The day you stood before the LORD your God at Horeb, the LORD said to me, “Gather the people before Me to hear My words, so that they may learn to fear Me all the days they live on the earth, and that they may teach them to their children.”

verses.kjv: And ye came near and stood under the mountain; and the mountain burned with fire unto the midst of heaven, with darkness, clouds, and thick darkness.
verses.bsb: You came near and stood at the base of the mountain, a mountain blazing with fire to the heavens, with black clouds and deep darkness.

verses.kjv: And the Lord spake unto you out of the midst of the fire: ye heard the voice of the words, but saw no similitude; only ye heard a voice.
verses.bsb: And the LORD spoke to you out of the fire. You heard the sound of the words, but saw no form; there was only a voice.

verses.kjv: And he declared unto you his covenant, which he commanded you to perform, even ten commandments; and he wrote them upon two tables of stone.
verses.bsb: He declared to you His covenant, which He commanded you to follow—the Ten Commandments that He wrote on two tablets of stone.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord commanded me at that time to teach you statutes and judgments, that ye might do them in the land whither ye go over to possess it.
verses.bsb: At that time the LORD commanded me to teach you the statutes and ordinances you are to follow in the land that you are crossing the Jordan to possess.

verses.kjv: Take ye therefore good heed unto yourselves; for ye saw no manner of similitude on the day that the Lord spake unto you in Horeb out of the midst of the fire:
verses.bsb: So since you saw no form of any kind on the day the LORD spoke to you out of the fire at Horeb, be careful

verses.kjv: Lest ye corrupt yourselves, and make you a graven image, the similitude of any figure, the likeness of male or female,
verses.bsb: that you do not act corruptly and make an idol for yourselves of any form or shape, whether in the likeness of a male or female,

verses.kjv: The likeness of any beast that is on the earth, the likeness of any winged fowl that flieth in the air,
verses.bsb: of any beast that is on the earth or bird that flies in the air,

verses.kjv: The likeness of any thing that creepeth on the ground, the likeness of any fish that is in the waters beneath the earth:
verses.bsb: or of any creature that crawls on the ground or fish that is in the waters below.

verses.kjv: And lest thou lift up thine eyes unto heaven, and when thou seest the sun, and the moon, and the stars, even all the host of heaven, shouldest be driven to worship them, and serve them, which the Lord thy God hath divided unto all nations under the whole heaven.
verses.bsb: When you look to the heavens and see the sun and moon and stars—all the host of heaven—do not be enticed to bow down and worship what the LORD your God has apportioned to all the nations under heaven.

verses.kjv: But the Lord hath taken you, and brought you forth out of the iron furnace, even out of Egypt, to be unto him a people of inheritance, as ye are this day.
verses.bsb: Yet the LORD has taken you and brought you out of the iron furnace, out of Egypt, to be the people of His inheritance, as you are today.

verses.kjv: Furthermore the Lord was angry with me for your sakes, and sware that I should not go over Jordan, and that I should not go in unto that good land, which the Lord thy God giveth thee for an inheritance:
verses.bsb: The LORD, however, was angry with me on account of you, and He swore that I would not cross the Jordan to enter the good land that the LORD your God is giving you as an inheritance.

verses.kjv: But I must die in this land, I must not go over Jordan: but ye shall go over, and possess that good land.
verses.bsb: For I will not be crossing the Jordan, because I must die in this land. But you shall cross over and take possession of that good land.

verses.kjv: Take heed unto yourselves, lest ye forget the covenant of the Lord your God, which he made with you, and make you a graven image, or the likeness of any thing, which the Lord thy God hath forbidden thee.
verses.bsb: Be careful that you do not forget the covenant of the LORD your God that He made with you; do not make an idol for yourselves in the form of anything He has forbidden you.

verses.kjv: For the Lord thy God is a consuming fire, even a jealous God.
verses.bsb: For the LORD your God is a consuming fire, a jealous God.

verses.kjv: ¶ When thou shalt beget children, and children’s children, and ye shall have remained long in the land, and shall corrupt yourselves, and make a graven image, or the likeness of any thing, and shall do evil in the sight of the Lord thy God, to provoke him to anger:
verses.bsb: After you have children and grandchildren and you have been in the land a long time, if you then act corruptly and make an idol of any form—doing evil in the sight of the LORD your God and provoking Him to anger—

verses.kjv: I call heaven and earth to witness against you this day, that ye shall soon utterly perish from off the land whereunto ye go over Jordan to possess it; ye shall not prolong your days upon it, but shall utterly be destroyed.
verses.bsb: I call heaven and earth as witnesses against you this day that you will quickly perish from the land that you are crossing the Jordan to possess. You will not live long upon it, but will be utterly destroyed.

verses.kjv: And the Lord shall scatter you among the nations, and ye shall be left few in number among the heathen, whither the Lord shall lead you.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD will scatter you among the peoples, and only a few of you will survive among the nations to which the LORD will drive you.

verses.kjv: And there ye shall serve gods, the work of men’s hands, wood and stone, which neither see, nor hear, nor eat, nor smell.
verses.bsb: And there you will serve man-made gods of wood and stone, which cannot see or hear or eat or smell.

verses.kjv: But if from thence thou shalt seek the Lord thy God, thou shalt find him, if thou seek him with all thy heart and with all thy soul.
verses.bsb: But if from there you will seek the LORD your God, you will find Him if you seek Him with all your heart and with all your soul.

verses.kjv: When thou art in tribulation, and all these things are come upon thee, even in the latter days, if thou turn to the Lord thy God, and shalt be obedient unto his voice;
verses.bsb: When you are in distress and all these things have happened to you, then in later days you will return to the LORD your God and listen to His voice.

verses.kjv: (For the Lord thy God is a merciful God;) he will not forsake thee, neither destroy thee, nor forget the covenant of thy fathers which he sware unto them.
verses.bsb: For the LORD your God is a merciful God; He will not abandon you or destroy you or forget the covenant with your fathers, which He swore to them by oath.

verses.kjv: For ask now of the days that are past, which were before thee, since the day that God created man upon the earth, and ask from the one side of heaven unto the other, whether there hath been any such thing as this great thing is, or hath been heard like it?
verses.bsb: Indeed, ask now from one end of the heavens to the other about the days that long preceded you, from the day that God created man on earth: Has anything as great as this ever happened or been reported?

verses.kjv: Did ever people hear the voice of God speaking out of the midst of the fire, as thou hast heard, and live?
verses.bsb: Has a people ever heard the voice of God speaking out of the fire, as you have, and lived?

verses.kjv: Or hath God assayed to go and take him a nation from the midst of another nation, by temptations, by signs, and by wonders, and by war, and by a mighty hand, and by a stretched out arm, and by great terrors, according to all that the Lord your God did for you in Egypt before your eyes?
verses.bsb: Or has any god tried to take as his own a nation out of another nation—by trials, signs, wonders, and war, by a strong hand and an outstretched arm, and by great terrors—as the LORD your God did for you in Egypt, before your eyes?

verses.kjv: Unto thee it was shewed, that thou mightest know that the Lord he is God; there is none else beside him.
verses.bsb: You were shown these things so that you would know that the LORD is God; there is no other besides Him.

verses.kjv: Out of heaven he made thee to hear his voice, that he might instruct thee: and upon earth he shewed thee his great fire; and thou heardest his words out of the midst of the fire.
verses.bsb: He let you hear His voice from heaven to discipline you, and on earth He showed you His great fire, and you heard His words out of the fire.

verses.kjv: And because he loved thy fathers, therefore he chose their seed after them, and brought thee out in his sight with his mighty power out of Egypt;
verses.bsb: Because He loved your fathers, He chose their descendants after them and brought you out of Egypt by His presence and great power,

verses.kjv: To drive out nations from before thee greater and mightier than thou art, to bring thee in, to give thee their land for an inheritance, as it is this day.
verses.bsb: to drive out before you nations greater and mightier than you, and to bring you into their land and give it to you for your inheritance, as it is this day.

verses.kjv: Know therefore this day, and consider it in thine heart, that the Lord he is God in heaven above, and upon the earth beneath: there is none else.
verses.bsb: Know therefore this day and take to heart that the LORD is God in heaven above and on the earth below; there is no other.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt keep therefore his statutes, and his commandments, which I command thee this day, that it may go well with thee, and with thy children after thee, and that thou mayest prolong thy days upon the earth, which the Lord thy God giveth thee, for ever.
verses.bsb: Keep His statutes and commandments, which I am giving you today, so that you and your children after you may prosper, and that you may live long in the land that the LORD your God is giving you for all time.

verses.kjv: ¶ Then Moses severed three cities on this side Jordan toward the sunrising;
verses.bsb: Then Moses set aside three cities across the Jordan to the east

verses.kjv: That the slayer might flee thither, which should kill his neighbour unawares, and hated him not in times past; and that fleeing unto one of these cities he might live:
verses.bsb: to which a manslayer could flee after killing his neighbor unintentionally without prior malice.  To save one’s own life, he could flee to one of these cities:

verses.kjv: Namely, Bezer in the wilderness, in the plain country, of the Reubenites; and Ramoth in Gilead, of the Gadites; and Golan in Bashan, of the Manassites.
verses.bsb: Bezer in the wilderness on the plateau belonging to the Reubenites, Ramoth in Gilead belonging to the Gadites, or Golan in Bashan belonging to the Manassites.

verses.kjv: ¶ And this is the law which Moses set before the children of Israel:
verses.bsb: This is the law that Moses set before the Israelites.

verses.kjv: These are the testimonies, and the statutes, and the judgments, which Moses spake unto the children of Israel, after they came forth out of Egypt,
verses.bsb: These are the testimonies, statutes, and ordinances that Moses proclaimed to them after they had come out of Egypt,

verses.kjv: On this side Jordan, in the valley over against Beth-peor, in the land of Sihon king of the Amorites, who dwelt at Heshbon, whom Moses and the children of Israel smote, after they were come forth out of Egypt:
verses.bsb: while they were in the valley across the Jordan facing Beth-peor in the land of Sihon king of the Amorites, who lived in Heshbon and was defeated by Moses and the Israelites after they had come out of Egypt.

verses.kjv: And they possessed his land, and the land of Og king of Bashan, two kings of the Amorites, which were on this side Jordan toward the sunrising;
verses.bsb: They took possession of the land belonging to Sihon and to Og king of Bashan—the two Amorite kings across the Jordan to the east—

verses.kjv: From Aroer, which is by the bank of the river Arnon, even unto mount Sion, which is Hermon,
verses.bsb: extending from Aroer on the rim of the Arnon Valley as far as Mount Siyon (that is, Hermon),

verses.kjv: And all the plain on this side Jordan eastward, even unto the sea of the plain, under the springs of Pisgah.
verses.bsb: including all the Arabah on the east side of the Jordan and as far as the Sea of the Arabah, below the slopes of Pisgah.

verses.kjv: AND Moses called all Israel, and said unto them, Hear, O Israel, the statutes and judgments which I speak in your ears this day, that ye may learn them, and keep, and do them.
verses.bsb: Then Moses summoned all Israel and said to them:  Hear, O Israel, the statutes and ordinances that I declare in your hearing this day. Learn them and observe them carefully.

verses.kjv: The Lord our God made a covenant with us in Horeb.
verses.bsb: The LORD our God made a covenant with us at Horeb.

verses.kjv: The Lord made not this covenant with our fathers, but with us, even us, who are all of us here alive this day.
verses.bsb: He did not make this covenant with our fathers, but with all of us who are alive here today.

verses.kjv: The Lord talked with you face to face in the mount out of the midst of the fire,
verses.bsb: The LORD spoke with you face to face out of the fire on the mountain.

verses.kjv: (I stood between the Lord and you at that time, to shew you the word of the Lord: for ye were afraid by reason of the fire, and went not up into the mount;) saying,
verses.bsb: At that time I was standing between the LORD and you to declare to you the word of the LORD, because you were afraid of the fire and would not go up the mountain. And He said:

verses.kjv: ¶ I am the Lord thy God, which brought thee out of the land of Egypt, from the house of bondage.
verses.bsb: “I am the LORD your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt have none other gods before me.
verses.bsb: You shall have no other gods before Me.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not make thee any graven image, or any likeness of any thing that is in heaven above, or that is in the earth beneath, or that is in the waters beneath the earth:
verses.bsb: You shall not make for yourself an idol in the form of anything in the heavens above, on the earth below, or in the waters beneath.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not bow down thyself unto them, nor serve them: for I the Lord thy God am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth generation of them that hate me,
verses.bsb: You shall not bow down to them or worship them; for I, the LORD your God, am a jealous God, visiting the iniquity of the fathers on their children to the third and fourth generations of those who hate Me,

verses.kjv: And shewing mercy unto thousands of them that love me and keep my commandments.
verses.bsb: but showing loving devotion to a thousand generations of those who love Me and keep My commandments.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord thy God in vain: for the Lord will not hold him guiltless that taketh his name in vain.
verses.bsb: You shall not take the name of the LORD your God in vain, for the LORD will not leave anyone unpunished who takes His name in vain.

verses.kjv: Keep the sabbath day to sanctify it, as the Lord thy God hath commanded thee.
verses.bsb: Observe the Sabbath day by keeping it holy, as the LORD your God has commanded you.

verses.kjv: Six days thou shalt labour, and do all thy work:
verses.bsb: Six days you shall labor and do all your work,

verses.kjv: But the seventh day is the sabbath of the Lord thy God: in it thou shalt not do any work, thou, nor thy son, nor thy daughter, nor thy manservant, nor thy maidservant, nor thine ox, nor thine ass, nor any of thy cattle, nor thy stranger that is within thy gates; that thy manservant and thy maidservant may rest as well as thou.
verses.bsb: but the seventh day is a Sabbath to the LORD your God, on which you must not do any work—neither you, nor your son or daughter, nor your manservant or maidservant, nor your ox or donkey or any of your livestock, nor the foreigner within your gates, so that your manservant and maidservant may rest as you do.

verses.kjv: And remember that thou wast a servant in the land of Egypt, and that the Lord thy God brought thee out thence through a mighty hand and by a stretched out arm: therefore the Lord thy God commanded thee to keep the sabbath day.
verses.bsb: Remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt, and that the LORD your God brought you out of there with a mighty hand and an outstretched arm. That is why the LORD your God has commanded you to keep the Sabbath day.

verses.kjv: ¶ Honour thy father and thy mother, as the Lord thy God hath commanded thee; that thy days may be prolonged, and that it may go well with thee, in the land which the Lord thy God giveth thee.
verses.bsb: Honor your father and your mother, as the LORD your God has commanded you, so that your days may be long and that it may go well with you in the land that the LORD your God is giving you.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not kill.
verses.bsb: You shall not murder.

verses.kjv: Neither shalt thou commit adultery.
verses.bsb: You shall not commit adultery.

verses.kjv: Neither shalt thou steal.
verses.bsb: You shall not steal.

verses.kjv: Neither shalt thou bear false witness against thy neighbour.
verses.bsb: You shall not bear false witness against your neighbor.

verses.kjv: Neither shalt thou desire thy neighbour’s wife, neither shalt thou covet thy neighbour’s house, his field, or his manservant, or his maidservant, his ox, or his ass, or any thing that is thy neighbour’s.
verses.bsb: You shall not covet your neighbor’s wife. You shall not covet your neighbor’s house or field, or his manservant or maidservant, or his ox or donkey, or anything that belongs to your neighbor.”

verses.kjv: ¶ These words the Lord spake unto all your assembly in the mount out of the midst of the fire, of the cloud, and of the thick darkness, with a great voice: and he added no more. And he wrote them in two tables of stone, and delivered them unto me.
verses.bsb: The LORD spoke these commandments in a loud voice to your whole assembly out of the fire, the cloud, and the deep darkness on the mountain; He added nothing more. And He wrote them on two tablets of stone and gave them to me.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when ye heard the voice out of the midst of the darkness, (for the mountain did burn with fire,) that ye came near unto me, even all the heads of your tribes, and your elders;
verses.bsb: And when you heard the voice out of the darkness while the mountain was blazing with fire, all the heads of your tribes and your elders approached me,

verses.kjv: And ye said, Behold, the Lord our God hath shewed us his glory and his greatness, and we have heard his voice out of the midst of the fire: we have seen this day that God doth talk with man, and he liveth.
verses.bsb: and you said, “Behold, the LORD our God has shown us His glory and greatness, and we have heard His voice out of the fire. Today we have seen that a man can live even if God speaks with him.

verses.kjv: Now therefore why should we die? for this great fire will consume us: if we hear the voice of the Lord our God any more, then we shall die.
verses.bsb: But now, why should we die? For this great fire will consume us, and we will die, if we hear the voice of the LORD our God any longer.

verses.kjv: For who is there of all flesh, that hath heard the voice of the living God speaking out of the midst of the fire, as we have, and lived?
verses.bsb: For who of all flesh has heard the voice of the living God speaking out of the fire, as we have, and survived?

verses.kjv: Go thou near, and hear all that the Lord our God shall say: and speak thou unto us all that the Lord our God shall speak unto thee; and we will hear it, and do it.
verses.bsb: Go near and listen to all that the LORD our God says. Then you can tell us everything the LORD our God tells you; we will listen and obey.”

verses.kjv: And the Lord heard the voice of your words, when ye spake unto me; and the Lord said unto me, I have heard the voice of the words of this people, which they have spoken unto thee: they have well said all that they have spoken.
verses.bsb: And the LORD heard the words you spoke to me, and He said to me, “I have heard the words that these people have spoken to you. They have done well in all that they have spoken.

verses.kjv: O that there were such an heart in them, that they would fear me, and keep all my commandments always, that it might be well with them, and with their children for ever!
verses.bsb: If only they had such a heart to fear Me and keep all My commandments always, so that it might be well with them and with their children forever.

verses.kjv: Go say to them, Get you into your tents again.
verses.bsb: Go and tell them: ‘Return to your tents.’

verses.kjv: But as for thee, stand thou here by me, and I will speak unto thee all the commandments, and the statutes, and the judgments, which thou shalt teach them, that they may do them in the land which I give them to possess it.
verses.bsb: But you stand here with Me, that I may speak to you all the commandments and statutes and ordinances you are to teach them to follow in the land that I am giving them to possess.”

verses.kjv: Ye shall observe to do therefore as the Lord your God hath commanded you: ye shall not turn aside to the right hand or to the left.
verses.bsb: So be careful to do as the LORD your God has commanded you; you are not to turn aside to the right or to the left.

verses.kjv: Ye shall walk in all the ways which the Lord your God hath commanded you, that ye may live, and that it may be well with you, and that ye may prolong your days in the land which ye shall possess.
verses.bsb: You must walk in all the ways that the LORD your God has commanded you, so that you may live and prosper and prolong your days in the land that you will possess.

verses.kjv: NOW these are the commandments, the statutes, and the judgments, which the Lord your God commanded to teach you, that ye might do them in the land whither ye go to possess it:
verses.bsb: These are the commandments and statutes and ordinances that the LORD your God has instructed me to teach you to follow in the land that you are about to enter and possess,

verses.kjv: That thou mightest fear the Lord thy God, to keep all his statutes and his commandments, which I command thee, thou, and thy son, and thy son’s son, all the days of thy life; and that thy days may be prolonged.
verses.bsb: so that you and your children and grandchildren may fear the LORD your God all the days of your lives by keeping all His statutes and commandments that I give you, and so that your days may be prolonged.

verses.kjv: ¶ Hear therefore, O Israel, and observe to do it; that it may be well with thee, and that ye may increase mightily, as the Lord God of thy fathers hath promised thee, in the land that floweth with milk and honey.
verses.bsb: Hear, O Israel, and be careful to observe them, so that you may prosper and multiply greatly in a land flowing with milk and honey, just as the LORD, the God of your fathers, has promised you.

verses.kjv: Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God is one Lord:
verses.bsb: Hear, O Israel: The LORD our God, the LORD is One.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt love the Lord thy God with all thine heart, and with all thy soul, and with all thy might.
verses.bsb: And you shall love the LORD your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your strength.

verses.kjv: And these words, which I command thee this day, shall be in thine heart:
verses.bsb: These words I am commanding you today are to be upon your hearts.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt teach them diligently unto thy children, and shalt talk of them when thou sittest in thine house, and when thou walkest by the way, and when thou liest down, and when thou risest up.
verses.bsb: And you shall teach them diligently to your children and speak of them when you sit at home and when you walk along the road, when you lie down and when you get up.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt bind them for a sign upon thine hand, and they shall be as frontlets between thine eyes.
verses.bsb: Tie them as reminders on your hands and bind them on your foreheads.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt write them upon the posts of thy house, and on thy gates.
verses.bsb: Write them on the doorposts of your houses and on your gates.

verses.kjv: And it shall be, when the Lord thy God shall have brought thee into the land which he sware unto thy fathers, to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, to give thee great and goodly cities, which thou buildedst not,
verses.bsb: And when the LORD your God brings you into the land He swore to your fathers, to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, that He would give you—a land with great and splendid cities that you did not build,

verses.kjv: And houses full of all good things, which thou filledst not, and wells digged, which thou diggedst not, vineyards and olive trees, which thou plantedst not; when thou shalt have eaten and be full;
verses.bsb: with houses full of every good thing with which you did not fill them, with wells that you did not dig, and with vineyards and olive groves that you did not plant—and when you eat and are satisfied,

verses.kjv: Then beware lest thou forget the Lord, which brought thee forth out of the land of Egypt, from the house of bondage.
verses.bsb: be careful not to forget the LORD who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt fear the Lord thy God, and serve him, and shalt swear by his name.
verses.bsb: Fear the LORD your God, serve Him only, and take your oaths in His name.

verses.kjv: Ye shall not go after other gods, of the gods of the people which are round about you;
verses.bsb: Do not follow other gods, the gods of the peoples around you.

verses.kjv: (For the Lord thy God is a jealous God among you) lest the anger of the Lord thy God be kindled against thee, and destroy thee from off the face of the earth.
verses.bsb: For the LORD your God, who is among you, is a jealous God. Otherwise the anger of the LORD your God will be kindled against you, and He will wipe you off the face of the earth.

verses.kjv: ¶ Ye shall not tempt the Lord your God, as ye tempted him in Massah.
verses.bsb: Do not test the LORD your God as you tested Him at Massah.

verses.kjv: Ye shall diligently keep the commandments of the Lord your God, and his testimonies, and his statutes, which he hath commanded thee.
verses.bsb: You are to diligently keep the commandments of the LORD your God and the testimonies and statutes He has given you.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt do that which is right and good in the sight of the Lord: that it may be well with thee, and that thou mayest go in and possess the good land which the Lord sware unto thy fathers,
verses.bsb: Do what is right and good in the sight of the LORD, so that it may be well with you and that you may enter and possess the good land that the LORD your God swore to give your fathers,

verses.kjv: To cast out all thine enemies from before thee, as the Lord hath spoken.
verses.bsb: driving out all your enemies before you, as the LORD has said.

verses.kjv: And when thy son asketh thee in time to come, saying, What mean the testimonies, and the statutes, and the judgments, which the Lord our God hath commanded you?
verses.bsb: In the future, when your son asks, “What is the meaning of the decrees and statutes and ordinances that the LORD our God has commanded you?”

verses.kjv: Then thou shalt say unto thy son, We were Pharaoh’s bondmen in Egypt; and the Lord brought us out of Egypt with a mighty hand:
verses.bsb: then you are to tell him, “We were slaves of Pharaoh in Egypt, but the LORD brought us out of Egypt with a mighty hand.

verses.kjv: And the Lord shewed signs and wonders, great and sore, upon Egypt, upon Pharaoh, and upon all his household, before our eyes:
verses.bsb: Before our eyes the LORD inflicted great and devastating signs and wonders on Egypt, on Pharaoh, and on all his household.

verses.kjv: And he brought us out from thence, that he might bring us in, to give us the land which he sware unto our fathers.
verses.bsb: But He brought us out from there to lead us in and give us the land that He had sworn to our fathers.

verses.kjv: And the Lord commanded us to do all these statutes, to fear the Lord our God, for our good always, that he might preserve us alive, as it is at this day.
verses.bsb: And the LORD commanded us to observe all these statutes and to fear the LORD our God, that we may always be prosperous and preserved, as we are to this day.

verses.kjv: And it shall be our righteousness, if we observe to do all these commandments before the Lord our God, as he hath commanded us.
verses.bsb: And if we are careful to observe every one of these commandments before the LORD our God, as He has commanded us, then that will be our righteousness.”

verses.kjv: WHEN the Lord thy God shall bring thee into the land whither thou goest to possess it, and hath cast out many nations before thee, the Hittites, and the Girgashites, and the Amorites, and the Canaanites, and the Perizzites, and the Hivites, and the Jebusites, seven nations greater and mightier than thou;
verses.bsb: When the LORD your God brings you into the land that you are entering to possess, and He drives out before you many nations—the Hittites, Girgashites, Amorites, Canaanites, Perizzites, Hivites, and Jebusites, seven nations larger and stronger than you—

verses.kjv: And when the Lord thy God shall deliver them before thee; thou shalt smite them, and utterly destroy them; thou shalt make no covenant with them, nor shew mercy unto them:
verses.bsb: and when the LORD your God has delivered them over to you to defeat them, then you must devote them to complete destruction. Make no treaty with them and show them no mercy.

verses.kjv: Neither shalt thou make marriages with them; thy daughter thou shalt not give unto his son, nor his daughter shalt thou take unto thy son.
verses.bsb: Do not intermarry with them. Do not give your daughters to their sons or take their daughters for your sons,

verses.kjv: For they will turn away thy son from following me, that they may serve other gods: so will the anger of the Lord be kindled against you, and destroy thee suddenly.
verses.bsb: because they will turn your sons away from following Me to serve other gods. Then the anger of the LORD will burn against you, and He will swiftly destroy you.

verses.kjv: But thus shall ye deal with them; ye shall destroy their altars, and break down their images, and cut down their groves, and burn their graven images with fire.
verses.bsb: Instead, this is what you are to do to them: tear down their altars, smash their sacred pillars, cut down their Asherah poles, and burn their idols in the fire.

verses.kjv: For thou art an holy people unto the Lord thy God: the Lord thy God hath chosen thee to be a special people unto himself, above all people that are upon the face of the earth.
verses.bsb: For you are a people holy to the LORD your God. The LORD your God has chosen you to be a people for His prized possession out of all peoples on the face of the earth.

verses.kjv: The Lord did not set his love upon you, nor choose you, because ye were more in number than any people; for ye were the fewest of all people:
verses.bsb: The LORD did not set His affection on you and choose you because you were more numerous than the other peoples, for you were the fewest of all peoples.

verses.kjv: But because the Lord loved you, and because he would keep the oath which he had sworn unto your fathers, hath the Lord brought you out with a mighty hand, and redeemed you out of the house of bondmen, from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.
verses.bsb: But because the LORD loved you and kept the oath He swore to your fathers, He brought you out with a mighty hand and redeemed you from the house of slavery, from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.

verses.kjv: Know therefore that the Lord thy God, he is God, the faithful God, which keepeth covenant and mercy with them that love him and keep his commandments to a thousand generations;
verses.bsb: Know therefore that the LORD your God is God, the faithful God who keeps His covenant of loving devotion for a thousand generations of those who love Him and keep His commandments.

verses.kjv: And repayeth them that hate him to their face, to destroy them: he will not be slack to him that hateth him, he will repay him to his face.
verses.bsb: But those who hate Him He repays to their faces with destruction; He will not hesitate to repay to his face the one who hates Him.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt therefore keep the commandments, and the statutes, and the judgments, which I command thee this day, to do them.
verses.bsb: So keep the commandments and statutes and ordinances that I am giving you to follow this day.

verses.kjv: ¶ Wherefore it shall come to pass, if ye hearken to these judgments, and keep, and do them, that the Lord thy God shall keep unto thee the covenant and the mercy which he sware unto thy fathers:
verses.bsb: If you listen to these ordinances and keep them carefully, then the LORD your God will keep His covenant and the loving devotion that He swore to your fathers.

verses.kjv: And he will love thee, and bless thee, and multiply thee: he will also bless the fruit of thy womb, and the fruit of thy land, thy corn, and thy wine, and thine oil, the increase of thy kine, and the flocks of thy sheep, in the land which he sware unto thy fathers to give thee.
verses.bsb: He will love you and bless you and multiply you. He will bless the fruit of your womb and the produce of your land—your grain, new wine, and oil, the young of your herds and the lambs of your flocks—in the land that He swore to your fathers to give you.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt be blessed above all people: there shall not be male or female barren among you, or among your cattle.
verses.bsb: You will be blessed above all peoples; among you there will be no barren man or woman or livestock.

verses.kjv: And the Lord will take away from thee all sickness, and will put none of the evil diseases of Egypt, which thou knowest, upon thee; but will lay them upon all them that hate thee.
verses.bsb: And the LORD will remove from you all sickness. He will not lay upon you any of the terrible diseases you knew in Egypt, but He will inflict them on all who hate you.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt consume all the people which the Lord thy God shall deliver thee; thine eye shall have no pity upon them: neither shalt thou serve their gods; for that will be a snare unto thee.
verses.bsb: You must destroy all the peoples the LORD your God will deliver to you. Do not look on them with pity. Do not worship their gods, for that will be a snare to you.

verses.kjv: If thou shalt say in thine heart, These nations are more than I; how can I dispossess them?
verses.bsb: You may say in your heart, “These nations are greater than we are; how can we drive them out?”

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not be afraid of them: but shalt well remember what the Lord thy God did unto Pharaoh, and unto all Egypt;
verses.bsb: But do not be afraid of them. Be sure to remember what the LORD your God did to Pharaoh and all Egypt:

verses.kjv: The great temptations which thine eyes saw, and the signs, and the wonders, and the mighty hand, and the stretched out arm, whereby the Lord thy God brought thee out: so shall the Lord thy God do unto all the people of whom thou art afraid.
verses.bsb: the great trials that you saw, the signs and wonders, and the mighty hand and outstretched arm by which the LORD your God brought you out. The LORD your God will do the same to all the peoples you now fear.

verses.kjv: Moreover the Lord thy God will send the hornet among them, until they that are left, and hide themselves from thee, be destroyed.
verses.bsb: Moreover, the LORD your God will send the hornet against them until even the survivors hiding from you have perished.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt not be affrighted at them: for the Lord thy God is among you, a mighty God and terrible.
verses.bsb: Do not be terrified by them, for the LORD your God, who is among you, is a great and awesome God.

verses.kjv: And the Lord thy God will put out those nations before thee by little and little: thou mayest not consume them at once, lest the beasts of the field increase upon thee.
verses.bsb: The LORD your God will drive out these nations before you little by little. You will not be enabled to eliminate them all at once, or the wild animals would multiply around you.

verses.kjv: But the Lord thy God shall deliver them unto thee, and shall destroy them with a mighty destruction, until they be destroyed.
verses.bsb: But the LORD your God will give them over to you and throw them into great confusion, until they are destroyed.

verses.kjv: And he shall deliver their kings into thine hand, and thou shalt destroy their name from under heaven: there shall no man be able to stand before thee, until thou have destroyed them.
verses.bsb: He will hand their kings over to you, and you will wipe out their names from under heaven. No one will be able to stand against you; you will annihilate them.

verses.kjv: The graven images of their gods shall ye burn with fire: thou shalt not desire the silver or gold that is on them, nor take it unto thee, lest thou be snared therein: for it is an abomination to the Lord thy God.
verses.bsb: You must burn up the images of their gods; do not covet the silver and gold that is on them or take it for yourselves, or you will be ensnared by it; for it is detestable to the LORD your God.

verses.kjv: Neither shalt thou bring an abomination into thine house, lest thou be a cursed thing like it: but thou shalt utterly detest it, and thou shalt utterly abhor it; for it is a cursed thing.
verses.bsb: And you must not bring any detestable thing into your house, or you, like it, will be set apart for destruction. You are to utterly detest and abhor it, because it is set apart for destruction.

verses.kjv: ALL the commandments which I command thee this day shall ye observe to do, that ye may live, and multiply, and go in and possess the land which the Lord sware unto your fathers.
verses.bsb: You must carefully follow every commandment I am giving you today, so that you may live and multiply, and enter and possess the land that the LORD swore to give your fathers.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt remember all the way which the Lord thy God led thee these forty years in the wilderness, to humble thee, and to prove thee, to know what was in thine heart, whether thou wouldest keep his commandments, or no.
verses.bsb: Remember that these forty years the LORD your God led you all the way in the wilderness, so that He might humble you and test you in order to know what was in your heart, whether or not you would keep His commandments.

verses.kjv: And he humbled thee, and suffered thee to hunger, and fed thee with manna, which thou knewest not, neither did thy fathers know; that he might make thee know that man doth not live by bread only, but by every word that proceedeth out of the mouth of the Lord doth man live.
verses.bsb: He humbled you, and in your hunger He gave you manna to eat, which neither you nor your fathers had known, so that you might understand that man does not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of the LORD.

verses.kjv: Thy raiment waxed not old upon thee, neither did thy foot swell, these forty years.
verses.bsb: Your clothing did not wear out and your feet did not swell during these forty years.

verses.kjv: Thou shalt also consider in thine heart, that, as a man chasteneth his son, so the Lord thy God chasteneth thee.
verses.bsb: So know in your heart that just as a man disciplines his son, so the LORD your God disciplines you.

verses.kjv: Therefore thou shalt keep the commandments of the Lord thy God, to walk in his ways, and to fear him.
verses.bsb: Therefore you shall keep the commandments of the LORD your God, walking in His ways and fearing Him.

verses.kjv: For the Lord thy God bringeth thee into a good land, a land of brooks of water, of fountains and depths that spring out of valleys and hills;
verses.bsb: For the LORD your God is bringing you into a good land, a land of brooks and fountains and springs that flow through the valleys and hills;

verses.kjv: A land of wheat, and barley, and vines, and fig trees, and pomegranates; a land of oil olive, and honey;
verses.bsb: a land of wheat, barley, vines, fig trees, and pomegranates; a land of olive oil and honey;

verses.kjv: A land wherein thou shalt eat bread without scarceness, thou shalt not lack any thing in it; a land whose stones are iron, and out of whose hills thou mayest dig brass.
verses.bsb: a land where you will eat food without scarcity, where you will lack nothing; a land whose rocks are iron and whose hills are ready to be mined for copper.

verses.kjv: When thou hast eaten and art full, then thou shalt bless the Lord thy God for the good land which he hath given thee.
verses.bsb: When you eat and are satisfied, you are to bless the LORD your God for the good land that He has given you.

verses.kjv: Beware that thou forget not the Lord thy God, in not keeping his commandments, and his judgments, and his statutes, which I command thee this day:
verses.bsb: Be careful not to forget the LORD your God by failing to keep His commandments and ordinances and statutes, which I am giving you this day.

verses.kjv: Lest when thou hast eaten and art full, and hast built goodly houses, and dwelt therein;
verses.bsb: Otherwise, when you eat and are satisfied, when you build fine houses in which to dwell,

verses.kjv: And when thy herds and thy flocks multiply, and thy silver and thy gold is multiplied, and all that thou hast is multiplied;
verses.bsb: and when your herds and flocks grow large and your silver and gold increase and all that you have is multiplied,

verses.kjv: Then thine heart be lifted up, and thou forget the Lord thy God, which brought thee forth out of the land of Egypt, from the house of bondage;
verses.bsb: then your heart will become proud, and you will forget the LORD your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.
